document_info.prepared_by: David Reinhardt, Vice President, Transportation Insurance Specialists

business_description.nature_of_business: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors

coverages_discussed.notes: Workers' Compensation coverage is provided through Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation per state statute. 24 claims, $826,622.56 paid, $896,295.98 incurred over review period.
Occurrence form; includes standard premises/operations and products/completed operations. 5 claims, $54,126.51 paid, $54,126.51 incurred over review period.
Covers all owned, hired, and non-owned vehicles. 16 claims, $387,376.46 paid, $449,550.02 incurred over review period.

coverages_discussed.requested_limit: Special form with replacement cost valuation for all scheduled locations
coverages_discussed.notes: 2 claims, $106,691.36 paid, $106,691.36 incurred over review period.
Following form over primary GL, Auto, and Employer's Liability. 0 claims, $0.00 paid, $0.00 incurred over review period.
1 claim, $3,916.24 paid, $3,916.24 incurred over review period.

positives: The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually.
The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits.
An employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative have been in place for over five years.
The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements.
A third-party risk control survey was completed within the past 12 months with favorable results.
The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%.
All locations are equipped with security camera systems and 24/7 monitoring services.
With 485 employees and a structured HR program, the insured maintains low turnover and consistent safety training.
The insured's fleet of 37 vehicles is maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule.
concerns: Mixed loss trend with some large individual claims in the review period.
13 claims remain open out of 48 total.
Annotation on document indicates supporting documents are needed ('Need docs').
Date discrepancy: document header dated 03/15/2026 but signature area shows date 6/18/2025.
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over the review period (5 loss years), the insured has incurred 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 in total paid losses and $1,510,580.11 total incurred. Currently, 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% (avg annual incurred $302,116.02 against current annual premium $2,333,947.33). Breakdown by coverage: Workers' Compensation — 24 claims, $826,622.56 paid, $896,295.98 incurred; General Liability — 5 claims, $54,126.51 paid, $54,126.51 incurred; Commercial Auto — 16 claims, $387,376.46 paid, $449,550.02 incurred; Commercial Property — 2 claims, $106,691.36 paid, $106,691.36 incurred; Umbrella/Excess — 0 claims, $0.00 paid, $0.00 incurred; Motor Truck Cargo — 1 claim, $3,916.24 paid, $3,916.24 incurred.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Great Lakes Freight Systems, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, with approximately 38 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property, Umbrella / Excess, Motor Truck Cargo.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, OH. Fleet of 37 vehicles. 485 employees.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. An employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative have been in place for over five years. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. A third-party risk control survey was completed within the past 12 months with favorable results. The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. All locations are equipped with security camera systems and 24/7 monitoring services. With 485 employees and a structured HR program, the insured maintains low turnover and consistent safety training. The insured's fleet of 37 vehicles is maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 in total paid losses and $1,510,580.11 total incurred. Currently, 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% (avg annual incurred $302,116.02 against current annual premium $2,333,947.33 over 5 loss years).
